2606.04262.title: Can I Take Another Dose? Evaluating LLM Decision-Making Under Temporal Uncertainty in OTC Dosing QA
2606.04262.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used for everyday health questions, including whether a user can safely take another dose of an over-the-counter (OTC) medication. Yet this common safety-relevant setting remains underexplored in existing medical QA evaluations, where correct answers require tracking dose timing, computing rolling 24-hour intake, following product-label constraints, and handling incomplete medication histories. We introduce DOSEBENCH, a focused benchmark of 81 curated OTC dosing scenarios focused on adult acetaminophen and ibuprofen use, with manually annotated gold references. We evaluate four LLMs across repeated runs using metrics for decision correctness, consistency, explanation verifiability, failure types, and confidence-related signals, resulting in 1,620 model responses. Our results show that models frequently struggle with rolling-window reasoning and ambiguity-sensitive cases and that stable or confident-looking responses can still violate dosing constraints. These findings suggest that OTC dosing QA provides a narrow yet practical testbed for evaluating temporal reasoning, constraint following, and safety-relevant uncertainty handling in medical QA.
2606.04262.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越多地用於日常健康問題，包括用戶是否可以安全地服用另一劑非處方藥（OTC）。<lb/>然而，這個常見的安全相關場景在現有的醫療問答評估中仍然未受到充分探索，正確的答案需要追蹤劑量時間、計算滾動的24小時攝取量、遵循產品標籤的限制，並處理不完整的用藥歷史。<lb/>我們介紹了DOSEBENCH，一個專注於81個精心策劃的OTC劑量場景的基準，重點關注成人對乙醯氨基酚和布洛芬的使用，並附有手動標註的金標準參考。<lb/>我們在重複運行中評估了四個LLMs，使用決策正確性、一致性、解釋可驗證性、失敗類型和信心相關信號的指標，總共產生了1,620個模型回應。<lb/>我們的結果顯示，模型在滾動窗口推理和對模糊情況的敏感性方面經常遇到困難，而穩定或看起來自信的回應仍然可能違反劑量限制。<lb/>這些發現表明，OTC劑量問答提供了一個狹窄但實用的測試平台，用於評估醫療問答中的時間推理、約束遵循和安全相關的不確定性處理。
2606.04262.author: Maroof Kousar et.al.
